pages.raw_text:
M. +. Hurley, roadmaster on the
Great Northern division on which
occurred the. Milan wreck, is report.
ed to have resigned and will accept
a position with the o. r. & N. Ry.


Mr.. Hurley testified before the
railway commission that he had
appropriated" 1900 ties from an
other railway In order to keep Jim
Hills line from going to pieces.
Someone was needed as Escape
goat" for the rotten condition of the
Great Northern roadbed and Hurley
appears to be the victim.


Ordinarily any man who could
annex 1900 ties and get away with
1t would appear to be too valuable
man to release In this manner,
especially in Hills service, but that
may be only small number of ties
to swlpe compared with what other
employees of Jim Hill get away
with, and then 1900 ties may be a
high water mark on the Hartman
lines and account for the readiness
with which Mr.. Hurley secured a
position with the o. r. & N. This
railroad game is getting wildly in
teresting and varied these days.
